message:
bd-xkcj: pane bookmarks + aliases (schema v20)

Add pane_bookmarks table with alias uniqueness, optional tags (JSON array),
and description. Storage layer: insert, delete, query-by-alias, list-all,
list-by-tag. CLI: `wa panes bookmark add/list/remove` with --json support.

5 new tests: insert+query, alias uniqueness, list+delete, tag filtering,
persistence across restarts. All quality gates green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-wlqi","title":"Create missing JSON schemas for robot commands","description":"## Goal
Create JSON schemas for all new robot mode commands.

## Schemas to Create
1. wa-robot-why.json - Response for `wa robot why`
2. wa-robot-approve.json - Response for `wa robot approve`
3. wa-robot-workflow-list.json - Response for `wa robot workflow list`
4. wa-robot-workflow-status.json - Response for `wa robot workflow status`
5. wa-robot-workflow-abort.json - Response for `wa robot workflow abort`

## Location
docs/json-schema/

## Acceptance Criteria
- All schemas follow existing patterns (use $defs, proper descriptions)
- Schemas validate against actual command output
- Schemas document all fields including optional ones","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:22:05.218460936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:38.858266253Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:38.858214435Z","close_reason":"Implemented: JSON schemas for why, approve, workflow-list, workflow-status, workflow-abort","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wlqi","depends_on_id":"bd-h1ai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}

- Dry-run output is stable and redacted","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:11:09.624253699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:54:40.943867667Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ybyi","depends_on_id":"bd-19i4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ybyi","depends_on_id":"bd-31qb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ybyi","depends_on_id":"wa-y6g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-yt9x","title":"Design saved search model + scheduling semantics","description":"## What
Define the saved search schema (query, filters, scope), storage location, and scheduling semantics.

## Why
A clear model prevents CLI/TUI/web divergence and ensures alerts are deterministic.

## How
- Specify fields (name, query, pane scope, filters, cadence, enabled flag, last_run_at)
- Define schedule resolution (fixed interval, no cron yet)
- Define alert payload shape (redacted preview + counts)

## Risks
- Overlapping with existing search explainability; ensure reuse of core search APIs.

## Success Criteria
- Schema and scheduling contract documented in this bead
- Downstream tasks can implement without guesswork","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:20.194559968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:40:25.310043105Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T03:40:25.309960873Z","close_reason":"Draft spec in docs/saved-searches-spec.md","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yt9x","depends_on_id":"bd-1t2b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-01x","title":"Version compatibility check: detect incompatible wa/database combinations","description":"# Task: Version Compatibility Check

## Goal
Prevent users from accidentally running incompatible wa versions against their database.

## Scenarios
1. **Newer wa, older DB**: Migration needed (usually safe)
2. **Older wa, newer DB**: DANGEROUS - wa cannot read newer schema
3. **Major version jump**: May require manual intervention

## Implementation

### Database Version Tracking
Track schema_version, min_compatible_wa, created_by_wa, created_at in wa_meta table.

### Startup Check
On open, verify:
- current_wa >= min_compatible_wa (else: WaTooOld error)
- schema_version <= supported_schema (else: SchemaTooNew error)
- schema_version == supported_schema (else: MigrationNeeded)

### User-Facing Messages
Old wa + new DB: Clear error with upgrade instructions
New wa + old DB: Offer migration with backup

## Testing
- Unit tests: version comparison logic
- Integration: simulate version mismatches
- E2E: upgrade/downgrade scenarios

## Acceptance Criteria
- Old wa refuses to open newer DB with clear error
- New wa offers migration for older DB
- Version info tracked in database metadata
- Helpful upgrade instructions provided","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:58:00.500288986Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.706395892Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:55.705890700Z","close_reason":"Added wa_meta version compatibility checks + schema gating","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-storage","area-tests","area-ux","phase-1","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.11","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf.5","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-upg.7","title":"[EPIC] Prepare/commit approvals with plan-hash binding (TOCTOU-safe)","description":"# [EPIC] Prepare/commit approvals with plan-hash binding

## Mission
Make approvals safer and more ergonomic by splitting unsafe actions into:
- **prepare**: compute and display the ActionPlan + deterministic hash
- **commit**: execute exactly that plan only if the user approved the hash

This prevents a common failure mode:
- user approves "something" but the action changes before execution (TOCTOU)

## Why This Matters
Approval UX is a trust boundary. We want:
- explicit, reviewable intent
- low confusion
- auditability
- minimal chance of approving the wrong thing

## Scope
- Extend allow-once approvals to bind to a **plan hash**.
- Add CLI flows:
  - `wa prepare ...` (or `--dry-run --prepare`)
  - `wa commit <hash>`
- Robot/MCP flows return:
  - plan
  - plan_hash
  - approval instructions

## Success Criteria
- Users approve a plan hash, not a vague action.
- Commits are rejected if the plan differs or has expired.
- Audit trail records prepare/approve/commit chain.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - plan hash stability
  - approval binding correctness
  - rejection on mismatch
- E2E:
  - prepare → approve → commit succeeds
  - prepare → plan changes → commit denied with actionable reason

## Acceptance Criteria
- Prepare/commit flow exists for at least SendText and workflow execution.
- Approvals are scoped, expiring, and auditable.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:43:47.442173439Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:40:39.888159516Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-audit","area-safety","area-tests","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.7","depends_on_id":"bd-24o0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:47Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.7.1","title":"Design: prepare/commit UX + plan-hash binding semantics","description":"# Task: Design prepare/commit approvals

## Goal
Specify the prepare/commit UX and the exact semantics of plan-hash binding.

## Requirements
- UX flows:
  - human CLI: prepare shows plan + hash, commit executes
  - robot: returns structured prepare result with remediation
  - MCP: same contract as robot
- Binding semantics:
  - approvals bind to:
    - workspace
    - plan_hash
    - action kind(s)
    - target pane_uuid (when applicable)
    - TTL
  - commit must refuse execution if:
    - plan hash mismatch
    - TTL expired
    - target pane identity changed
- Errors:
  - stable error codes and actionable remediation

## Acceptance Criteria
- The design prevents TOCTOU and confused-deputy approval mistakes.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyMeadow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:44:02.143171953Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-30T06:28:31.481757194Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T06:28:31.481691341Z","close_reason":"Documented prepare/commit UX, plan-hash binding semantics, and error codes in docs/action-plan-schema.md.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-design","area-safety","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.7.1","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:47Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.7.2","title":"Implement wa prepare/commit commands (plan preview + hash-bound execution)","description":"# Task: Implement `wa prepare` / `wa commit`

## Goal
Implement prepare/commit commands (or flags) that expose ActionPlans and execute them only when approved.

## Requirements
- `wa prepare <action>`:
  - computes ActionPlan
  - prints plan and hash
  - returns machine output via `--format json`
- `wa commit <plan_hash>`:
  - looks up prepared plan (or accepts plan payload)
  - checks approval binding
  - executes plan with durable step logs

## Testing
- CLI contract tests for JSON output.
- Unit tests for reject cases (unknown hash, expired, mismatch).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Prepare output is stable and usable.
- Commit refuses unsafe/mismatched execution with actionable errors.

 {"id":"wa-upg.7.4","title":"Audit chain: record prepare→approve→commit with correlation IDs","description":"# Task: Audit prepare/approve/commit chain

## Goal
Make approvals auditable and explainable by recording the full chain.

## Requirements
- Record events:
  - prepare created (plan_hash, summary)
  - approval granted (who, TTL, scope)
  - commit executed (success/failure, step logs)
- Correlation:
  - all entries share a correlation id
- Redaction:
  - never store secrets in plan or audit

## Testing
- Integration tests validate audit entries and redaction.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a plan_hash, users can trace the full approval/execution history.

 {"id":"wa-upg.7.5","title":"Tests: prepare/commit hash binding + mismatch rejection","description":"# Task: Tests for prepare/commit approvals

## Goal
Ensure prepare/commit approvals are correct and hard to misuse.

## Requirements
- Unit tests:
  - plan hash stability
  - commit rejects when plan differs
  - commit rejects when TTL expired
  - commit rejects when scope mismatched (workspace/pane_uuid)
- Output tests:
  - stable error codes and remediation text

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests would catch any regression that allows committing an unapproved plan.

 {"id":"wa-upg.7.6","title":"E2E: prepare→approve→commit succeeds; mismatch commit denied","description":"# Task: E2E prepare/commit approvals

## Goal
Prove prepare/commit approvals work end-to-end and prevent TOCTOU errors.

## Scenario A (happy path)
- Run `wa prepare ...` for a gated action.
- Approve the plan hash.
- Run `wa commit <hash>` and verify success.

## Scenario B (mismatch)
- Prepare a plan.
- Change the underlying inputs so the recomputed plan differs.
- Attempt commit and verify:
  - denied
  - actionable reason (hash mismatch)

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use wait-for/quiescence.
- Artifacts include:
  - prepare JSON
  - approval grant record
  - commit result + step logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E demonstrates both correctness and safety properties.
